description: Universal placement rules extracted from 27 Elektor schematics (2005-2023)

universal_rules.description: 100% of analyzed schematics use left-to-right signal flow
universal_rules.application: Input connectors/sensors at left edge, output connectors/actuators at right edge

universal_rules.description: 96% use V+ at top, GND at bottom. Symmetric supplies use V+ top, GND center, V- bottom.
universal_rules.application: Power rails as horizontal lines, positive higher, negative lower

universal_rules.description: Main processing IC (timer, MCU, opamp) placed center-left, rx 0.25-0.45
universal_rules.application: Place the most-connected IC at center-left, passives radiate outward

universal_rules.description: Timing R/C placed directly adjacent to IC pins they connect to
universal_rules.application: Bypass caps near VCC pin, timing R/C near oscillator pins, feedback R near FB pin

universal_rules.description: All connectors placed at sheet edges — input left, output right, power top-left
universal_rules.application: Never place connectors in the middle of the schematic

universal_rules.description: Transistor/MOSFET drivers between control IC and actuator, at rx 0.6-0.8
universal_rules.application: Driver stage always to the RIGHT of the controlling IC

universal_rules.description: R and C components oriented vertically (90 degrees) in 90% of cases
universal_rules.application: Default rotation for R, C, L, D = 90 degrees

universal_rules.description: Multi-unit ICs (quad opamp, dual timer) have units stacked vertically
universal_rules.application: IC.A at top, IC.B below, IC.C below that, etc.

universal_rules.description: Feedback paths from output wrap back to input via bottom of schematic
universal_rules.application: Route feedback wires below the main signal path

universal_rules.description: When showing circuit variants or application circuits, stack vertically
universal_rules.application: Primary circuit in top half, variant/application in bottom half
